# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.

Write secure code.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: 
    name: linkerd-service   # Name of the service to be created in Kubernetes cluster, e.g., microservices_communication_security
specs:    
 - type: LoadBalancer      # Expose your services as a load balancing solution (e.g.: cloudflare) 
    ports:                  # Define which port(s)/protocol to expose the service on  
        - name: http          # Name of exposed Port, e.g., web-app or api    
            protocol: TCP      # Protocol used for communication with clients (e.g.: HTTP/TCP) 
            port: 80           # Exposed port number in the cluster  
        - name: https          # Name of exposed Port, e.g., web-app or api    
            protocol: TCP      # Protocol used for communication with clients (e.g.: HTTP/TCP)   
            port: 443           # Exposed port number in the cluster  
---      
apiVersion: networking.linkerd.io/v1alpha2        # Linkerd specific API version, e.g., v0.9 or latest    
kind: VirtualService         # Type of resource to create (e.g.: Service) 
metadata:                     # Metadata for the service  
    name: microservices-communication-security      # Name given to this virtualservice in Kubernetes cluster, e.g., linkerd_inject    
specs:                        # Specifications of resource (e.g.: Service) 
    - http:                      # HTTP traffic specification  
        route:                     # Route definition for the service's requests and responses to/from other services in your mesh, e.g., microservices-communication_security    
            destinations:           # Destination definitions (e.g.: webapp) 
                - name: myservice    # Name of destination Service  
                  namespace: default      # Namespace where the service is deployed on Kubernetes cluster, e.g., microservices-communication_security    
        tls:                        # TLS settings for secure communication with other services in your mesh (e.g.: myservice) 
            mode: Terminate           # Mode of termination when a client connection fails or times out  
    - weighted                  # Weighted traffic specification, e.g., microservices-communication_security    
        destination:              # Destination definition for the service's requests and responses to/from other services in your mesh (e.g.: myservice) 
            name: myservice         # Name of Service  
    - retryAttempts             # Retry attempts specification, e.g., microservices-communication_security    
        attemptDelay: "10m"       # Delay between each failed request (e.g.: after a 5 minute delay)
